name: Kshitij
description: High-throughput compact transformer with Muon optimizer, EMA, flash attention, sliding-window validation, and int8 quantization
notes: This submission implements multiple advanced techniques: Muon optimizer with Newton-Schulz orthogonalization, int8 post-training quantization with per-row scaling and zlib compression, tokenizer-agnostic BPB evaluation, U-Net style encoder-decoder transformer with skip connections, tied embeddings with custom LR, RMSNorm + rotary embeddings + GQA, SwiGLU MLP, distributed token streaming loader, EMA weight averaging, AdamW-style decoupled weight decay, gradient clipping, cosine warmup-warmdown LR schedule, flash attention, and sliding-window validation. Hyperparameters are passed via environment variables (visible in logs). Current run was executed on 1x H100 due to resource constraints; full intended configuration and performance scaling require 8x H100 SXM to maximize throughput, achieve lower BPB, and fully utilize distributed training optimizations.
